items.title: Expressions, operators and conversions: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Overloading an operator changes its precedence and evaluation grouping for the class.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

answer.explanation: Operator precedence and arity remain fixed by C++ grammar; overloading selects function behavior for the already-grouped operands.

items.title: Expressions, operators and conversions: executable application
items.prompt: Read one integer x, evaluate the grouped C++ expression x multiplied by two and increased by one, and print only the resulting integer.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
